adapterBoundary.policy: Thin plugin, hook, MCP, and agent-host adapters can be the product boundary; keep them unless host registration proof says they are redundant.

gainHonesty.policy: Benchmark impact is not a current-repo savings claim without a matched baseline.

hardwareCalibration.policy: Hardware, sensors, clocks, timing, and physical devices need calibration or tuning proof before simplification.

oneRunnableCheck.policy: Non-trivial new logic should leave one smallest runnable check; trivial one-liners do not need ceremony.

requestedExplanation.policy: Explicitly requested reports, walkthroughs, phase notes, and rationale are not clutter.

shortcutDebt.policy: Every intentional shortcut needs a ceiling and upgrade trigger.

evidence.snippet: shipguard-lean marker with a ceiling and upgrade trigger

findings.proofGuidance: Keep the marker until repeated-key support is required, then replace the parser and remove the shortcut.
findings.recommendation: Leave the tracked shortcut visible because it has a ceiling and upgrade trigger.

evidence.snippet: shipguard-lean marker with a ceiling but no upgrade trigger

findings.proofGuidance: Add an upgrade trigger before treating this shortcut as intentional debt.
findings.recommendation: Mark the shortcut needs-trigger until the report says when to replace it.

expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the leanDebtLedger actionability question in JSON and Markdown
the fixture exposes leanDebtLedger summary and markers in JSON
the fixture exposes Lean Debt Ledger with Ceiling and Upgrade Trigger columns in Markdown
the fixture keeps scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly explicit
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
fixtureCandidate.privateDataPolicy: This promoted fixture is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.
fixtureCandidate.sourceQuestion: Does leanDebtLedger make intentional shortcuts auditable with ceilings and upgrade triggers?

leanDebtLedger.description: Intentional lean shortcuts found in comments. Every shortcut should name a ceiling and an upgrade trigger so deferrals stay reviewable.

markers.summary: use the native parser here. ceiling: only one query string shape. upgrade: replace when repeated-key support is required.
markers.upgrade: replace when repeated-key support is required

markers.summary: cache the first fixture load while the preview dataset is tiny. ceiling: preview data only.

reportQualityQuestions: Does leanDebtLedger make intentional shortcuts auditable with ceilings and upgrade triggers?

scanScope.note: Synthetic fixture; no private source tree was scanned.

scopeBoundary.purpose: Synthetic public fixture for ShipGuard report-quality behavior.
